title: Attractor Factor
titleUz: Attractor Factor — nimaga eʼtibor bersang, oʻsha keladi

intro.uz: Attractor Factor — Joe Vitalening gʻoyasi: nimaga diqqat qaratsangiz, oʻsha narsani hayotingizga tortasiz. Salbiylikka emas, ijobiy maqsadga fokuslaning. Bu haftaning ikki grammatik mavzusi shu gʻoyaga mos: soʻnggi paytda hozirgina boshlangan yaxshi oʻzgarishlar (present perfect + just / already / yet / still) va yaxshilikni tortadigan shartlar (kengaytirilgan birinchi shart: unless / as long as / provided that / in case). Eve mushuk haqidagi POV hikoyada ikkalasini ham eshitasiz.
intro.en: The Attractor Factor is Joe Vitale's idea: you attract into your life whatever you focus on. Stop feeding the negative; focus on the positive goal. This week's two grammar topics match that idea: the good changes that have just started happening lately (present perfect + just / already / yet / still) and the conditions that draw good things in (extended first conditional: unless / as long as / provided that / in case). You'll hear both in the POV story about Eve the cat.

canDo.uz: Dars oxirida soʻnggi paytda oʻzgargan biror narsani toʻxtamasdan tasvirlab bera olasiz ("I have just… / I haven't … yet") va biror natijaning shartini aniq bogʻlovchilar bilan ayta olasiz ("as long as… / unless…").
canDo.en: By the end you can describe something that has changed lately without pausing ("I have just… / I haven't … yet") and state the condition behind a result with precise connectors ("as long as… / unless…").

grammar.titleUz: Present perfect + just / already / yet / still (+ recently / lately)
grammar.titleEn: Present perfect + just / already / yet / still
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Part 1 — talk about what has changed in your life recently
grammar.cefrCanDo: B1: I can say what I have just / already / not yet done and place the adverb correctly
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbekchada bu ishlarni koʻpincha oddiy oʻtgan zamon bilan aytamiz: "Endigina tugatdim", "Allaqachon oʻzgardi". Ingliz tilida natija hozir tirik boʻlgani uchun present perfect kerak: "I have just finished". "hozirgina" = just, "allaqachon" = already, "hali (yoʻq)" = yet, "hali ham" = still.
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ I already have changed. Have you finished already? (inkor/savolda) ✅ I have already changed. Have you finished yet? — just/already `have/has` bilan `V3` oʻrtasida; savol va inkorda esa jumla oxirida `yet` ishlatiladi.

examples.en: I have just started a positive routine.
She has already changed her focus.
I still haven't removed the negativity from my day.
examples.uz: Kunimdagi salbiylikni hali ham olib tashlaganim yoʻq.

exercises.prompt: I have ___ finished the vocabulary lesson.
exercises.hintUz: "hozirgina" → just; u have va V3 oʻrtasida turadi (have just finished)

exercises.prompt: Have you started your positive plan ___?
She has ___ attracted good things into her life.
exercises.hintUz: ijobiy jumlada "allaqachon" → already, have/has bilan V3 oʻrtasida

exercises.promptUz: Oʻzingiz haqingizda rost gap ayting (ovoz chiqarib): soʻnggi paytda oʻzgargan biror narsa — "I have just … . I have already … . I haven't … yet."
exercises.hintUz: have/has + V3; just/already — oʻrtada, yet — oxirida (savol/inkor)

grammar.titleUz: Kengaytirilgan birinchi shart: unless / as long as / provided that / in case
grammar.titleEn: First conditional extended — unless / as long as / provided / in case
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Part 3 — discuss conditions and consequences precisely
grammar.cefrCanDo: B1→B2: I can express a condition with unless / as long as / provided that / in case
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbekchada bu maʼnolarni koʻpincha bitta "-sa" qoʻshimchasi bilan beramiz: "tirishsang", "tirishmasang". Ingliz tili alohida bogʻlovchi tanlaydi va ular almashmaydi: unless = agar …maslar (if not), as long as = toki …turar ekan, provided that = sharti bilan, in case = har ehtimolga qarshi. Shart gapida kelasi zamon emas, present simple qoladi.
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ You won't succeed unless you don't try. If you will focus, you will attract it. ✅ You won't succeed unless you try. If you focus, you will attract it. — "unless" ichida allaqachon inkor bor (yana "don't" qoʻshmang); shart gapida "will" ishlatilmaydi.

examples.en: You won't attract good things unless you believe.
You will improve as long as you stay positive.
examples.uz: Toki ijobiy qolar ekansiz, oʻsib borasiz.

examples.en: Take an umbrella in case it rains.
examples.uz: Yomgʻir yogʻsa kerak boʻladi deb, soyabon oling.

exercises.prompt: You will attract good things ___ you stay positive.
exercises.hintUz: "toki …turar ekan" (shart davom etsa natija ham davom etadi) → as long as

exercises.prompt: ___ you change your focus, nothing will improve.
I'll write the goal on the wall ___ I forget it.
exercises.hintUz: ehtiyot chorasi ("unutib qoʻysam kerak boʻladi") → in case

exercises.promptUz: Oʻzingiz haqingizda rost gap ayting (ovoz chiqarib): "I will … as long as … . I won't … unless … ."
exercises.hintUz: shart gapida present simple; unless ichida inkor bor, yana qoʻshmang

vocab.uz: ishga tushirmoq, yoqmoq (avval oʻchiq boʻlgan narsani)
vocab.defEn: to turn something on so it starts working
vocab.example: We activate the attractor force with our thinking.

vocab.uz: roʻyobga chiqmoq; xayoldagi narsaning haqiqatga aylanishi
vocab.defEn: to become real; a thing that becomes real
vocab.example: My idea manifested — it became real.

vocab.uz: turtki, sakrash taxtasi (biror ishni kuch bilan boshlab beruvchi narsa)
vocab.defEn: something that gives energy and starts a process
vocab.example: Your dreams are a springboard to miracles.

vocab.uz: voz kechmoq, oʻzini tiymoq (isteʼmol qilmaslik, uzoqlashmoq)
vocab.defEn: to avoid or not use something on purpose
vocab.example: You need to abstain from negative influences.

vocab.defEn: to not allow something to happen
vocab.example: Jack Canfield forbade any negativity in his office.

vocab.defEn: people or things that affect you in a bad way
to give all your attention to something
vocab.example: Focus on what you do want, not what you don't.

vocab.defEn: the way things are for you right now

vocab.uz: (salbiy fikrga) berilib ketmoq, ixtiyorsiz tushib qolmoq
vocab.defEn: to slip into a bad habit or state without meaning to
vocab.example: It's easy to fall into negative thinking.

warmup.uz: Bu suhbatda er-xotin uy hayvoni olish ustida bahslashadi: kuchukmi, mushukmi yoki toʻtiqushmi? Attractor Factor darsining Eve mushugiga chiroyli ulanadi. Biri "yoʻq" desa, biri "ha" deb turadi — nimaga fokuslansa, oʻsha yutadi. Sizningcha, oxirida qaysi hayvonni olishadi?
warmup.en: In this dialogue a couple argue about getting a pet — a puppy, a cat, or a parrot? It links nicely to the Attractor Factor lesson and Eve the cat: one focuses on "no", the other on "yes". Which pet do you predict they end up with?

keyVocab.defEn: to take care of someone or something
to wash a person or an animal
a particular kind or type of animal, e.g. of dog

keyVocab.uz: shu yerdan davom ettirmoq, keyin koʻramiz deb boshlamoq
keyVocab.defEn: to continue on from a point once you have started

quiz.q: What percentage of Americans said they believe in love at first sight?
quiz.qUz: Amerikaliklarning necha foizi ilk koʻrishdayoq sevgiga ishonishini aytgan?
quiz.explanationUz: Toʻgʻri javob: b) 56%. Sophie 26% dedi va xato qildi. 2013-yilgi CBS.com soʻrovnomasiga koʻra amerikaliklarning 56% ilk koʻrishda sevgiga ishonadi, turmush qurganlar orasida bu raqam yanada yuqori.

vocab.en: a bolt out of the blue
vocab.uz: kutilmaganda boʻlgan voqea, momaqaldiroqdek kutilmagan narsa

vocab.defEn: the relationship between two sizes or amounts
to have a strong relationship with something
a curvy body shape with a well-defined waist
judging something by the way it looks

funEnglish.title: The happy secret to better work | Shawn Achor

speakingPrompt.en: Think of a positive change you want to attract into your life. What have you just started or not done yet? And what will happen — as long as you keep going, or unless you give up?
speakingPrompt.uz: Taxminan 60 soniya gapiring. Bu haftaning ikki grammatikasidan foydalaning: present perfect + just/already/yet/still (I have just…, I haven't … yet) va kengaytirilgan birinchi shart (as long as…, unless…).
